methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Preferred Networks is a Series D+ stage AI & Software company founded by Toru Nishikawa and Daisuke Okanohara that has raised JPY 24B+ (~$160M) to date. The relevant peer universe consists of 1 public comparable (Palantir) and 8 private peers including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks—a total of 9 datapoints spanning the frontier AI infrastructure and software sector.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The AI & Software sector contains 38 comparable companies, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow cohort. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, revenue maturity, and capital efficiency across the broader AI & Software universe. The limited narrow peer set reflects both the nascency of the sector and the scarcity of late-stage private AI infrastructure companies with disclosed valuations.

Without sufficient narrow comparables, triangulation cannot be reliably completed using standard multiples-based approaches. A credible valuation range would require either: (1) revenue or growth-rate based analysis if confidential financials are available to the analyst; (2) forward-looking DCF sensitivity anchored to reasonable margin and growth assumptions; or (3) explicit reliance on the disclosed $160M raised as a floor, with upside calibrated to the maturity and competitive positioning of named peers. The key uncertainty remains the absence of sufficiently granular peer data at Preferred Networks' specific stage and scale.
